questions.question: A too-high learning rate typically causes:
questions.explanation: High learning rates can overshoot minima, causing oscillation.

questions.question: Increasing ansatz depth generally:

options.text: Increases expressibility but may cause barren plateaus

questions.explanation: More depth means more expressibility but also trainability challenges.

questions.question: Random search can outperform grid search for hyperparameters.
questions.explanation: Random search is often more efficient for high-dimensional spaces.

questions.question: Shot allocation should typically:
questions.explanation: More shots early helps find the right direction before fine-tuning.
